heading: Example
body:
The following index definition indexes the `title` field in
the [sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection using a
custom analyzer named `keywordStemRemover`. The custom analyzer
specifies the following:

- Apply the [whitespace](whitespace-tokenizer-ref) tokenizer to
  create tokens based on occurrences of whitespace between words.

- Apply the following filters on the tokens:

  - [keywordRepeat](keywordRepeat-tf-ref) token filter to create
    tokens twice, once as a keyword token and once a non-keyword token.

  - [porterStemming](porterStemming-tf-ref) token filter to
    remove the common morphological and inflectional suffixes from the
    non-keyword tokens.

  - [removeDuplicates](removeDuplicates-tf-ref) to filter out
    tokens at the same position for the same term as the previous token
    in the stream.

Atlas UI

Visual Editor

- In the Custom Analyzers section, click Add
  Custom Analyzer.

- Select the Create Your Own radio button and click
  Next.

- Type whitespace in the Analyzer Name field.

- Expand Tokenizer if it's collapsed.

- Select whitespace from the dropdown.

- Expand Token Filters and click 
  Add token filter.

- Select lowercase from the dropdown and click
  Add token filter.

- Click  Add token filter to add
  the following token filters to your custom analyzer:

  - removeDuplicates

  - spanishPluralStemming

- Click Add token filter to add the token filter to your
  custom analyzer.

- Click Add to create the custom analyzer.

- In the Field Mappings section, click Add
  Field Mapping to apply the custom analyzer on
  the message field.

- Select message from the Field
  Name dropdown and dateFacet from the Data
  Type dropdown.

- In the properties section for the data type, select whitespace
  from the Index Analyzer and Search Analyzer
  dropdowns.

- Click Add, then Save Changes.

JSON Editor

Replace the default index definition with the following example:

```json
{
  "mappings": {
    "dynamic": false,
    "fields": {
      "title": {
        "analyzer": "keywordStemRemover",
        "type": "string"
      }
    }
  },
  "analyzers": [
    {
      "name": "keywordStemRemover",
      "tokenFilters": [
        {
          "type": "keywordRepeat"
        },
        {
          "type": "porterStemming"
        },
        {
          "type": "removeDuplicates"
        }
      ],
      "tokenizer": {
        "type": "whitespace"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

MongoDB Shell

The following query searches the `title` field in the
[sample collection](custom-analyzers-eg-coll) collection for the term
`mee*` using the [wildcard](wildcard-ref).

Atlas UI

MongoDB Shell

MongoDB Search returns the documents with the term `meeting` in the title field.
The `keywordRepeat` token filter generates the following tokens for
the documents in the results:

| Title                     | Keyword Tokens               | Non-Keyword tokens           |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| The team's weekly meeting | The, team's, weekly, meeting | The, team's, weekly, meeting |
| The regular board meeting | The, regular, board, meeting | The, regular, board, meeting |

The `porterStemming` token filter reduces the non-keyword tokens to
their root base forms:

| Title                     | Stemmed Non-Keyword Tokens |
| ------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| The team's weekly meeting | the, team, week, meet      |
| The regular board meeting | the, regular, board, meet  |

The `removeDuplicates` token filter removes any duplicate tokens in
the stream generated by a preceding `keywordRepeat` token filter:

| Title                     | Tokens                                         |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| The team's weekly meeting | the, team's, team, weekly, week, meeting, meet |
| The regular board meeting | the, regular, board, meeting, meet             |
